mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, which compresses short-term rates and anchors the 10-year yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward yield changes in easing periods are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the 6-month annualized growth rate of CPI exceeds 2%, the market prices in higher future inflation and monetary tightening, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If triggered signals produce yield declines > 50% of the time, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with declining unemployment increases market expectations of future inflation and monetary tightening, pushing the 10-year yield up over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized quarterly growth > 2.5% and UNRATE 3-month change < -0.2pp
failure_condition: When joint condition holds, 20-day yield changes are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.10) or hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime (UNRATE below 5.5%), an increase in nonfarm payrolls signals tightening labor market and upward wage pressure, leading to higher inflation expectations and a rise in the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If over any period when UNRATE < 5.5%, the correlation between PAYEMS changes and 20-day yield changes is not positive (p>0.05) or Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals robust economic activity, leading to higher real rates and thus an upward move in the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC of INDPRO (monthly change) vs 20-day yield change is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly surge in housing starts greater than 5% signals strong upcoming demand and economic momentum, pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If triggered signals produce yield declines more than 50% of times, or the average yield change is not positive (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Higher-than-expected GDP growth increases market expectations for future inflation and monetary policy tightening, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the correlation between GDPC1 growth (quarterly) and 60-day forward yield changes is not positive (p>0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is high and inflation is very low, deflationary pressures dominate, causing the 10-year yield to fall over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If when triggered, 20-day yield changes are not negative on average (one-sided t-test p>0.10) or hit rate below 55% for negative moves.
